title: Hyperglycemic Clamp Procedure in Mice
author: Li Kang
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.yxefxje

steps.title: Surgical catheterization of the carotid artery...
steps.instruction: Surgical catheterization of the carotid artery and jugular vein in mice at least 5 days prior to the day of the study (refer to protocol for Surgical Catheterization of the Carotid Artery and Jugular Vein).

steps.title: Weigh mouse and start fast (suggested...
steps.instruction: Weigh mouse and start fast (suggested starting time between 7:00 and 8:00 AM) by placing mouse in a plastic container with fresh bedding.

steps.title: Mouse is hooked up to the...
steps.instruction: Mouse is hooked up to the swivel 4 hours into fasting and basal blood/plasma samples are collected 15 and 5 mins before the start of the clamp (refer to protocol for Hyperinsulinemic-Euglycemic Clamp for detailed set-up and connections).

steps.title: After a total of 5 hr...
steps.instruction: After a total of 5 hr fast, variable infusion of 50% glucose starts. Arterial glucose is increased and maintained at 250-300 mg/dL.

steps.title: Donor blood is infused to jugular...
steps.instruction: Donor blood is infused to jugular vein catheter throughout the study to prevent a fall of hematocrit.

steps.title: Plasma insulin and C-Peptide concentrations are...
steps.instruction: Plasma insulin and C-Peptide concentrations are measured at times as described in the following study sheet.

steps.title: At the end of the study,...
steps.instruction: At the end of the study, mouse is anesthetized and tissues of interest are harvested and frozen in liquid nitrogen. I: sample for plasma insulin concentration (25 µl plasma) C: sample for C-peptide at t = -5,15,100, and 120 (50 µl blood)
